authors_text: Minghao Chen,Fangyun Wei,Chong Li,Deng Cai
title: Frame-wise Action Representations for Long Videos via Sequence Contrastive Learning
title_zh: 通过序列对比学习的长视频的逐帧动作表示
abstract: Prior works on action representation learning mainly focus on designing various architectures to extract the global representations for short video clips. In contrast, many practical applications such as video alignment have strong demand for learning dense representations for long videos. In this paper, we introduce a novel contrastive action representation learning (CARL) framework to learn frame-wise action representations, especially for long videos, in a self-supervised manner. Concretely, we introduce a simple yet efficient video encoder that considers spatio-temporal context to extract frame-wise representations. Inspired by the recent progress of self-supervised learning, we present a novel sequence contrastive loss (SCL) applied on two correlated views obtained through a series of spatio-temporal data augmentations. SCL optimizes the embedding space by minimizing the KL-divergence between the sequence similarity of two augmented views and a prior Gaussian distribution of timestamp distance. Experiments on FineGym, PennAction and Pouring datasets show that our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art by a large margin for downstream fine-grained action classification. Surprisingly, although without training on paired videos, our approach also shows outstanding performance on video alignment and fine-grained frame retrieval tasks. Code and models are available at https://github.com/minghchen/CARL_code.
summary: Prior works on action representation learning mainly focus on designing various architectures to extract the global representations for short video clips. In contrast, many practical applications such as video alignment have strong demand for learning dense representations for long videos. In this paper, we introduce a novel contrastive action representation learning (CARL) framework to learn frame-wise action representations, especially for long videos, in a self-supervised manner. Concretely, we introduce a simple yet efficient video encoder that considers spatio-temporal context to extract frame-wise representations. Inspired by the recent progress of self-supervised learning, we present a novel sequence contrastive loss (SCL) applied on two correlated views obtained through a series of spatio-temporal data augmentations. SCL optimizes the embedding space by minimizing the KL-divergence between the sequence similarity of two augmented views and a prior Gaussian distribution of timestamp distance. Experiments on FineGym, PennAction and Pouring datasets show that our method outperforms previous state-of-the-art by a large margin for downstream fine-grained action classification. Surprisingly, although without training on paired videos, our approach also shows outstanding performance on video alignment and fine-grained frame retrieval tasks. Code and models are available at https://github.com/minghchen/CARL_code.
abstract_zh: 先前关于动作表示学习的工作主要集中在设计各种架构来提取短视频剪辑的全局表示。相比之下，许多实际应用（例如视频对齐）对学习长视频的密集表示有强烈的需求。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的对比动作表示学习（CARL）框架，以自监督的方式学习逐帧动作表示，特别是长视频。具体来说，我们引入了一种简单而高效的视频编码器，它考虑时空上下文来提取逐帧表示。受自监督学习最新进展的启发，我们提出了一种新颖的序列对比损失（SCL），应用于通过一系列时空数据增强获得的两个相关视图。 SCL 通过最小化两个增强视图的序列相似性与时间戳距离的先验高斯分布之间的 KL 散度来优化嵌入空间。在 FineGym、PennAction 和 Pouring 数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法在下游细粒度动作分类方面大大优于以前的最先进技术。令人惊讶的是，尽管没有对配对视频进行训练，我们的方法在视频对齐和细粒度帧检索任务上也表现出了出色的性能。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/minghchen/CARL_code 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过序列对比学习为长视频生成帧级动作表示的框架CARL。
AI.motivation: 现有动作表示学习方法主要针对短视频剪辑的全局表示，而实际应用如视频对齐需要长视频的密集表示，现有方法难以满足需求。
AI.method: 设计考虑时空上下文的视频编码器，并引入序列对比损失（SCL），通过时空数据增强生成相关视图，利用KL散度优化嵌入空间以匹配时间戳距离的高斯先验分布。
AI.result: 在FineGym、PennAction和Pouring数据集上，CARL在细粒度动作分类任务中大幅超越现有方法，且在未使用配对视频训练的情况下，在视频对齐和帧检索任务中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: CARL通过自监督学习有效生成长视频帧级表示，在多个下游任务中表现突出，未来可进一步探索更精细的时序建模方法。

authors_text: Yi Wei,Zibu Wei,Yongming Rao,Jiaxin Li,Jie Zhou,Jiwen Lu
title: LiDAR Distillation: Bridging the Beam-Induced Domain Gap for 3D Object Detection
title_zh: LiDAR 蒸馏：弥合 3D 物体检测的光束诱导域间隙
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/weiyithu/LiDAR-Distillation
comments: Accepted to ECCV 2022. Code is available at https://github.com/weiyithu/LiDAR-Distillation
abstract: In this paper, we propose the LiDAR Distillation to bridge the domain gap induced by different LiDAR beams for 3D object detection. In many real-world applications, the LiDAR points used by mass-produced robots and vehicles usually have fewer beams than that in large-scale public datasets. Moreover, as the LiDARs are upgraded to other product models with different beam amount, it becomes challenging to utilize the labeled data captured by previous versions' high-resolution sensors. Despite the recent progress on domain adaptive 3D detection, most methods struggle to eliminate the beam-induced domain gap. We find that it is essential to align the point cloud density of the source domain with that of the target domain during the training process. Inspired by this discovery, we propose a progressive framework to mitigate the beam-induced domain shift. In each iteration, we first generate low-beam pseudo LiDAR by downsampling the high-beam point clouds. Then the teacher-student framework is employed to distill rich information from the data with more beams. Extensive experiments on Waymo, nuScenes and KITTI datasets with three different LiDAR-based detectors demonstrate the effectiveness of our LiDAR Distillation. Notably, our approach does not increase any additional computation cost for inference.
summary: In this paper, we propose the LiDAR Distillation to bridge the domain gap induced by different LiDAR beams for 3D object detection. In many real-world applications, the LiDAR points used by mass-produced robots and vehicles usually have fewer beams than that in large-scale public datasets. Moreover, as the LiDARs are upgraded to other product models with different beam amount, it becomes challenging to utilize the labeled data captured by previous versions' high-resolution sensors. Despite the recent progress on domain adaptive 3D detection, most methods struggle to eliminate the beam-induced domain gap. We find that it is essential to align the point cloud density of the source domain with that of the target domain during the training process. Inspired by this discovery, we propose a progressive framework to mitigate the beam-induced domain shift. In each iteration, we first generate low-beam pseudo LiDAR by downsampling the high-beam point clouds. Then the teacher-student framework is employed to distill rich information from the data with more beams. Extensive experiments on Waymo, nuScenes and KITTI datasets with three different LiDAR-based detectors demonstrate the effectiveness of our LiDAR Distillation. Notably, our approach does not increase any additional computation cost for inference.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了 LiDAR Distillation 来弥合不同 LiDAR 光束引起的域间隙，以进行 3D 物体检测。在许多现实应用中，批量生产的机器人和车辆使用的激光雷达点通常比大规模公共数据集中的光束更少。此外，随着激光雷达升级到不同光束量的其他产品型号，利用以前版本的高分辨率传感器捕获的标记数据变得具有挑战性。尽管域自适应 3D 检测最近取得了进展，但大多数方法都难以消除光束引起的域间隙。我们发现在训练过程中将源域的点云密度与目标域的点云密度保持一致至关重要。受这一发现的启发，我们提出了一个渐进式框架来减轻光束引起的域偏移。在每次迭代中，我们首先通过对远光点云进行下采样来生成近光伪 LiDAR。然后采用师生框架从更多波束的数据中提取丰富的信息。使用三种不同的基于 LiDAR 的探测器对 Waymo、nuScenes 和 KITTI 数据集进行大量实验，证明了我们的 LiDAR Distillation 的有效性。值得注意的是，我们的方法不会增加任何额外的推理计算成本。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LiDAR Distillation方法，解决因LiDAR光束差异导致的3D目标检测领域差距问题。
AI.motivation: 实际应用中量产机器人和车辆使用的LiDAR光束数少于公开数据集，且LiDAR升级后难以利用旧版本高分辨率传感器的标注数据，现有领域自适应方法难以消除光束引起的领域差距。
AI.method: 提出渐进式框架，在训练过程中通过下采样生成低光束伪LiDAR数据，利用教师-学生框架从高光束数据中蒸馏信息，逐步缩小领域差距。
AI.result: 在Waymo、nuScenes和KITTI数据集上进行大量实验，三种基于LiDAR的检测器均验证了方法有效性，且推理时无额外计算成本。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过点云密度对齐有效缓解光束引起的领域偏移，在保持推理效率的同时提升了跨光束配置的3D检测性能。

authors_text: Yang Xue,Yuheng Li,Krishna Kumar Singh,Yong Jae Lee
title: GIRAFFE HD: A High-Resolution 3D-aware Generative Model
title_zh: GIRAFFE HD：高分辨率 3D 感知生成模型
abstract: 3D-aware generative models have shown that the introduction of 3D information can lead to more controllable image generation. In particular, the current state-of-the-art model GIRAFFE can control each object's rotation, translation, scale, and scene camera pose without corresponding supervision. However, GIRAFFE only operates well when the image resolution is low. We propose GIRAFFE HD, a high-resolution 3D-aware generative model that inherits all of GIRAFFE's controllable features while generating high-quality, high-resolution images ($512^2$ resolution and above). The key idea is to leverage a style-based neural renderer, and to independently generate the foreground and background to force their disentanglement while imposing consistency constraints to stitch them together to composite a coherent final image. We demonstrate state-of-the-art 3D controllable high-resolution image generation on multiple natural image datasets.
summary: 3D-aware generative models have shown that the introduction of 3D information can lead to more controllable image generation. In particular, the current state-of-the-art model GIRAFFE can control each object's rotation, translation, scale, and scene camera pose without corresponding supervision. However, GIRAFFE only operates well when the image resolution is low. We propose GIRAFFE HD, a high-resolution 3D-aware generative model that inherits all of GIRAFFE's controllable features while generating high-quality, high-resolution images ($512^2$ resolution and above). The key idea is to leverage a style-based neural renderer, and to independently generate the foreground and background to force their disentanglement while imposing consistency constraints to stitch them together to composite a coherent final image. We demonstrate state-of-the-art 3D controllable high-resolution image generation on multiple natural image datasets.
abstract_zh: 3D 感知生成模型表明，引入 3D 信息可以导致更可控的图像生成。特别是，当前最先进的模型 GIRAFFE 可以在没有相应监督的情况下控制每个对象的旋转、平移、缩放和场景相机姿势。然而，GIRAFFE 仅在图像分辨率较低时才能正常运行。我们提出 GIRAFFE HD，一种高分辨率 3D 感知生成模型，继承了 GIRAFFE 的所有可控功能，同时生成高质量、高分辨率图像（分辨率为 512^2$ 及以上）。关键思想是利用基于风格的神经渲染器，独立生成前景和背景，迫使它们解开，同时施加一致性约束，将它们拼接在一起，合成一个连贯的最终图像。我们在多个自然图像数据集上演示了最先进的 3D 可控高分辨率图像生成。

AI.tldr: 本文提出GIRAFFE HD，一种能够生成高分辨率（512²及以上）且具有3D感知能力的生成模型，继承了GIRAFFE的可控特性。
AI.motivation: 现有模型GIRAFFE在低分辨率下表现良好，但无法生成高分辨率图像，限制了其应用。本文旨在解决这一问题，实现高分辨率与3D可控性的统一。
AI.method: 采用基于风格的神经渲染器，独立生成前景与背景并施加一致性约束，强制解耦并拼接为连贯图像。
AI.result: 在多个自然图像数据集上实现了高分辨率（512²及以上）3D可控图像生成的最先进性能，保持了GIRAFFE的可控特性。
AI.conclusion: GIRAFFE HD有效解决了高分辨率3D可控图像生成的难题，推动了该领域的发展。

search_text: GIRAFFE HD: A High-Resolution 3D-aware Generative Model GIRAFFE HD：高分辨率 3D 感知生成模型 3D-aware generative models have shown that the introduction of 3D information can lead to more controllable image generation. In particular, the current state-of-the-art model GIRAFFE can control each object's rotation, translation, scale, and scene camera pose without corresponding supervision. However, GIRAFFE only operates well when the image resolution is low. We propose GIRAFFE HD, a high-resolution 3D-aware generative model that inherits all of GIRAFFE's controllable features while generating high-quality, high-resolution images ($512^2$ resolution and above). The key idea is to leverage a style-based neural renderer, and to independently generate the foreground and background to force their disentanglement while imposing consistency constraints to stitch them together to composite a coherent final image. We demonstrate state-of-the-art 3D controllable high-resolution image generation on multiple natural image datasets. 3D 感知生成模型表明，引入 3D 信息可以导致更可控的图像生成。特别是，当前最先进的模型 GIRAFFE 可以在没有相应监督的情况下控制每个对象的旋转、平移、缩放和场景相机姿势。然而，GIRAFFE 仅在图像分辨率较低时才能正常运行。我们提出 GIRAFFE HD，一种高分辨率 3D 感知生成模型，继承了 GIRAFFE 的所有可控功能，同时生成高质量、高分辨率图像（分辨率为 512^2$ 及以上）。关键思想是利用基于风格的神经渲染器，独立生成前景和背景，迫使它们解开，同时施加一致性约束，将它们拼接在一起，合成一个连贯的最终图像。我们在多个自然图像数据集上演示了最先进的 3D 可控高分辨率图像生成。 本文提出GIRAFFE HD，一种能够生成高分辨率（512²及以上）且具有3D感知能力的生成模型，继承了GIRAFFE的可控特性。 Yang Xue,Yuheng Li,Krishna Kumar Singh,Yong Jae Lee cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: K J Joseph,Salman Khan,Fahad Shahbaz Khan,Rao Muhammad Anwer,Vineeth N Balasubramanian
title: Energy-based Latent Aligner for Incremental Learning
title_zh: 用于增量学习的基于能量的潜在对准器
comment: To appear in CVPR 2022. Code is available in https://github.com/JosephKJ/ELI
comments: To appear in CVPR 2022. Code is available in https://github.com/JosephKJ/ELI
abstract: Deep learning models tend to forget their earlier knowledge while incrementally learning new tasks. This behavior emerges because the parameter updates optimized for the new tasks may not align well with the updates suitable for older tasks. The resulting latent representation mismatch causes forgetting. In this work, we propose ELI: Energy-based Latent Aligner for Incremental Learning, which first learns an energy manifold for the latent representations such that previous task latents will have low energy and the current task latents have high energy values. This learned manifold is used to counter the representational shift that happens during incremental learning. The implicit regularization that is offered by our proposed methodology can be used as a plug-and-play module in existing incremental learning methodologies. We validate this through extensive evaluation on CIFAR-100, ImageNet subset, ImageNet 1k and Pascal VOC datasets. We observe consistent improvement when ELI is added to three prominent methodologies in class-incremental learning, across multiple incremental settings. Further, when added to the state-of-the-art incremental object detector, ELI provides over 5% improvement in detection accuracy, corroborating its effectiveness and complementary advantage to existing art.
summary: Deep learning models tend to forget their earlier knowledge while incrementally learning new tasks. This behavior emerges because the parameter updates optimized for the new tasks may not align well with the updates suitable for older tasks. The resulting latent representation mismatch causes forgetting. In this work, we propose ELI: Energy-based Latent Aligner for Incremental Learning, which first learns an energy manifold for the latent representations such that previous task latents will have low energy and the current task latents have high energy values. This learned manifold is used to counter the representational shift that happens during incremental learning. The implicit regularization that is offered by our proposed methodology can be used as a plug-and-play module in existing incremental learning methodologies. We validate this through extensive evaluation on CIFAR-100, ImageNet subset, ImageNet 1k and Pascal VOC datasets. We observe consistent improvement when ELI is added to three prominent methodologies in class-incremental learning, across multiple incremental settings. Further, when added to the state-of-the-art incremental object detector, ELI provides over 5% improvement in detection accuracy, corroborating its effectiveness and complementary advantage to existing art.
abstract_zh: 深度学习模型在逐步学习新任务时往往会忘记之前的知识。出现这种行为的原因是，针对新任务优化的参数更新可能与适合旧任务的更新不一致。由此产生的潜在表征不匹配会导致遗忘。在这项工作中，我们提出了 ELI：用于增量学习的基于能量的潜在对齐器，它首先学习潜在表示的能量流形，使得先前的任务潜在者将具有低能量，而当前任务潜在者具有高能量值。这种学习流形用于应对增量学习期间发生的表征转变。我们提出的方法提供的隐式正则化可以用作现有增量学习方法中的即插即用模块。我们通过对 CIFAR-100、ImageNet 子集、ImageNet 1k 和 Pascal VOC 数据集的广泛评估来验证这一点。当 ELI 添加到课堂增量学习的三种主要方法中时，我们观察到跨多个增量设置的持续改进。此外，当添加到最先进的增量物体检测器中时，ELI 的检测精度提高了 5% 以上，证实了其有效性以及与现有技术的互补优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于能量的潜在对齐器ELI，用于解决增量学习中的知识遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 深度学习模型在增量学习新任务时容易遗忘旧知识，因参数更新与旧任务需求不一致导致潜在表示不匹配。
AI.method: 通过学习能量流形使旧任务潜在表示具有低能量、新任务具有高能量，构建可插拔模块对抗表示偏移。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100、ImageNet等数据集上验证有效，提升检测准确率超5%，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: ELI方法有效缓解增量学习遗忘问题，可作为通用模块增强现有技术，具有显著互补优势。

authors_text: Dripta S. Raychaudhuri,Yumin Suh,Samuel Schulter,Xiang Yu,Masoud Faraki,Amit K. Roy-Chowdhury,Manmohan Chandraker
title: Controllable Dynamic Multi-Task Architectures
title_zh: 可控动态多任务架构
abstract: Multi-task learning commonly encounters competition for resources among tasks, specifically when model capacity is limited. This challenge motivates models which allow control over the relative importance of tasks and total compute cost during inference time. In this work, we propose such a controllable multi-task network that dynamically adjusts its architecture and weights to match the desired task preference as well as the resource constraints. In contrast to the existing dynamic multi-task approaches that adjust only the weights within a fixed architecture, our approach affords the flexibility to dynamically control the total computational cost and match the user-preferred task importance better. We propose a disentangled training of two hypernetworks, by exploiting task affinity and a novel branching regularized loss, to take input preferences and accordingly predict tree-structured models with adapted weights. Experiments on three multi-task benchmarks, namely PASCAL-Context, NYU-v2, and CIFAR-100, show the efficacy of our approach. Project page is available at https://www.nec-labs.com/~mas/DYMU.
summary: Multi-task learning commonly encounters competition for resources among tasks, specifically when model capacity is limited. This challenge motivates models which allow control over the relative importance of tasks and total compute cost during inference time. In this work, we propose such a controllable multi-task network that dynamically adjusts its architecture and weights to match the desired task preference as well as the resource constraints. In contrast to the existing dynamic multi-task approaches that adjust only the weights within a fixed architecture, our approach affords the flexibility to dynamically control the total computational cost and match the user-preferred task importance better. We propose a disentangled training of two hypernetworks, by exploiting task affinity and a novel branching regularized loss, to take input preferences and accordingly predict tree-structured models with adapted weights. Experiments on three multi-task benchmarks, namely PASCAL-Context, NYU-v2, and CIFAR-100, show the efficacy of our approach. Project page is available at https://www.nec-labs.com/~mas/DYMU.
abstract_zh: 多任务学习通常会遇到任务之间的资源竞争，特别是当模型容量有限时。这一挑战激发了模型允许在推理时间内控制任务的相对重要性和总计算成本。在这项工作中，我们提出了这样一个可控的多任务网络，它可以动态调整其架构和权重以匹配所需的任务偏好以及资源限制。与仅调整固定架构内的权重的现有动态多任务方法相比，我们的方法提供了动态控制总计算成本并更好地匹配用户首选任务重要性的灵活性。我们提出了两个超网络的分离训练，通过利用任务亲和力和新颖的分支正则化损失，来获取输入偏好并相应地预测具有调整权重的树结构模型。对三个多任务基准（即 PASCAL-Context、NYU-v2 和 CIFAR-100）的实验表明了我们方法的有效性。项目页面位于 https://www.nec-labs.com/~mas/DYMU。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可控的动态多任务网络架构，能够根据任务优先级和资源约束动态调整模型结构和权重。
AI.motivation: 多任务学习在模型容量有限时面临任务间资源竞争问题，现有动态方法仅调整固定架构内的权重，无法灵活控制计算成本与任务重要性匹配。
AI.method: 通过解耦训练两个超网络，结合任务亲和力和分支正则化损失，根据输入偏好预测树状结构模型并适配权重。
AI.result: 在PASCAL-Context、NYU-v2和CIFAR-100三个基准测试中验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过动态架构调整和权重预测，在资源约束下实现了更优的任务重要性匹配。

This challenge motivates models which allow control over the relative importance of tasks and total compute cost during inference time. In this work, we propose such a controllable multi-task network that dynamically adjusts its architecture and weights to match the desired task preference as well as the resource constraints. In contrast to the existing dynamic multi-task approaches that adjust only the weights within a fixed architecture, our approach affords the flexibility to dynamically control the total computational cost and match the user-preferred task importance better. We propose a disentangled training of two hypernetworks, by exploiting task affinity and a novel branching regularized loss, to take input preferences and accordingly predict tree-structured models with adapted weights. Experiments on three multi-task benchmarks, namely PASCAL-Context, NYU-v2, and CIFAR-100, show the efficacy of our approach. Project page is available at https://www.nec-labs.com/~mas/DYMU. 多任务学习通常会遇到任务之间的资源竞争，特别是当模型容量有限时。这一挑战激发了模型允许在推理时间内控制任务的相对重要性和总计算成本。在这项工作中，我们提出了这样一个可控的多任务网络，它可以动态调整其架构和权重以匹配所需的任务偏好以及资源限制。与仅调整固定架构内的权重的现有动态多任务方法相比，我们的方法提供了动态控制总计算成本并更好地匹配用户首选任务重要性的灵活性。我们提出了两个超网络的分离训练，通过利用任务亲和力和新颖的分支正则化损失，来获取输入偏好并相应地预测具有调整权重的树结构模型。对三个多任务基准（即 PASCAL-Context、NYU-v2 和 CIFAR-100）的实验表明了我们方法的有效性。项目页面位于 https://www.nec-labs.com/~mas/DYMU。 本文提出一种可控的动态多任务网络架构，能够根据任务优先级和资源约束动态调整模型结构和权重。 Dripta S. Raychaudhuri,Yumin Suh,Samuel Schulter,Xiang Yu,Masoud Faraki,Amit K. Roy-Chowdhury,Manmohan Chandraker cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Udith Haputhanthri,Andrew Seeber,Dushan Wadduwage
title: Differentiable Microscopy for Content and Task Aware Compressive Fluorescence Imaging
title_zh: 用于内容和任务感知压缩荧光成像的可微分显微镜
abstract: The trade-off between throughput and image quality is an inherent challenge in microscopy. To improve throughput, compressive imaging under-samples image signals; the images are then computationally reconstructed by solving a regularized inverse problem. Compared to traditional regularizers, Deep Learning based methods have achieved greater success in compression and image quality. However, the information loss in the acquisition process sets the compression bounds. Further improvement in compression, without compromising the reconstruction quality is thus a challenge. In this work, we propose differentiable compressive fluorescence microscopy ($\partial μ$) which includes a realistic generalizable forward model with learnable-physical parameters (e.g. illumination patterns), and a novel physics-inspired inverse model. The cascaded model is end-to-end differentiable and can learn optimal compressive sampling schemes through training data. With our model, we performed thousands of numerical experiments on various compressive microscope configurations. We show that learned sampling encodes important information about the specimens in the illumination field of the microscope allowing higher compression up to $\times 1024$. We further utilize our framework for Task Aware Compression. The experimental results show superior performance on the cell segmentation task.
summary: The trade-off between throughput and image quality is an inherent challenge in microscopy. To improve throughput, compressive imaging under-samples image signals; the images are then computationally reconstructed by solving a regularized inverse problem. Compared to traditional regularizers, Deep Learning based methods have achieved greater success in compression and image quality. However, the information loss in the acquisition process sets the compression bounds. Further improvement in compression, without compromising the reconstruction quality is thus a challenge. In this work, we propose differentiable compressive fluorescence microscopy ($\partial μ$) which includes a realistic generalizable forward model with learnable-physical parameters (e.g. illumination patterns), and a novel physics-inspired inverse model. The cascaded model is end-to-end differentiable and can learn optimal compressive sampling schemes through training data. With our model, we performed thousands of numerical experiments on various compressive microscope configurations. We show that learned sampling encodes important information about the specimens in the illumination field of the microscope allowing higher compression up to $\times 1024$. We further utilize our framework for Task Aware Compression. The experimental results show superior performance on the cell segmentation task.
abstract_zh: 吞吐量和图像质量之间的权衡是显微镜技术固有的挑战。为了提高吞吐量，压缩成像对图像信号进行欠采样；然后通过解决正则化反问题来计算重建图像。与传统的正则化器相比，基于深度学习的方法在压缩和图像质量方面取得了更大的成功。然而，采集过程中的信息丢失设定了压缩界限。因此，在不影响重建质量的情况下进一步改进压缩是一个挑战。在这项工作中，我们提出了可微分压缩荧光显微镜（$\partial μ$），其中包括具有可学习物理参数（例如照明模式）的现实可泛化正向模型，以及新颖的受物理启发的逆模型。级联模型是端到端可微的，可以通过训练数据学习最优压缩采样方案。通过我们的模型，我们对各种压缩显微镜配置进行了数千次数值实验。我们表明，学习采样对显微镜照明场中样本的重要信息进行编码，允许更高的压缩率，高达 $\times 1024$。我们进一步利用我们的任务感知压缩框架。实验结果显示在细胞分割任务上表现出优越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种可微分的压缩荧光显微镜方法（∂μ），通过结合可学习的物理参数和物理启发的逆模型，实现高倍压缩率下的高质量图像重建。
AI.motivation: 传统显微镜在吞吐量与图像质量间存在矛盾，压缩成像虽能提升效率但受限于信息丢失，现有深度学习方法难以进一步突破压缩上限。
AI.method: 构建端到端可微分模型，包含可学习物理参数的正向模型（如照明模式）与物理启发的逆模型，通过训练数据学习最优压缩采样方案，并实现任务感知压缩。
AI.result: 在多种显微镜配置下进行数千次数值实验，实现最高1024倍压缩率，且在细胞分割任务中表现优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过编码样本信息于照明场，突破传统压缩界限，在保持重建质量的同时显著提升压缩效率，验证了任务感知压缩的有效性。

authors_text: Kithmini Herath,Hasindu Kariyawasam,Ramith Hettiarachchi,Udith Haputhanthri,Dineth Jayakody,Raja N. Ahmad,Azeem Ahmad,Balpreet S. Ahluwalia,Chamira U. S. Edussooriya,Dushan N. Wadduwage
title: Differentiable Microscopy Designs an All Optical Phase Retrieval Microscope
title_zh: 可微分显微镜设计全光学相位检索显微镜
abstract: Designing new optical systems from the ground up for microscopy imaging tasks such as phase retrieval, requires substantial scientific expertise and creativity. To augment the traditional design process, we propose differentiable microscopy ($\partialμ$), which introduces a top-down design approach. Using all optical phase retrieval as an illustrative example, we demonstrate the effectiveness of data-driven microscopy design through $\partialμ$. Furthermore, we conduct comprehensive comparisons with existing all-optical phase retrieval methods, showcasing the consistent superiority of our learned designs across multiple datasets, including biological samples. To substantiate our ideas, we experimentally validate the functionality of one of the learned designs, providing a proof of concept. The proposed differentiable microscopy framework supplements the creative process of designing new phase microscopy systems and may be extended to other similar applications in optical design.
summary: Designing new optical systems from the ground up for microscopy imaging tasks such as phase retrieval, requires substantial scientific expertise and creativity. To augment the traditional design process, we propose differentiable microscopy ($\partialμ$), which introduces a top-down design approach. Using all optical phase retrieval as an illustrative example, we demonstrate the effectiveness of data-driven microscopy design through $\partialμ$. Furthermore, we conduct comprehensive comparisons with existing all-optical phase retrieval methods, showcasing the consistent superiority of our learned designs across multiple datasets, including biological samples. To substantiate our ideas, we experimentally validate the functionality of one of the learned designs, providing a proof of concept. The proposed differentiable microscopy framework supplements the creative process of designing new phase microscopy systems and may be extended to other similar applications in optical design.
abstract_zh: 从头开始设计用于显微镜成像任务（例如相位检索）的新光学系统，需要大量的科学专业知识和创造力。为了增强传统的设计过程，我们提出了可微分显微镜（$\partialμ$），它引入了自上而下的设计方法。使用全光学相位检索作为说明性示例，我们通过 $\partialμ$ 展示了数据驱动显微镜设计的有效性。此外，我们与现有的全光学相位检索方法进行了全面比较，展示了我们学习的设计在包括生物样本在内的多个数据集上的一致优越性。为了证实我们的想法，我们通过实验验证了所学设计之一的功能，提供了概念证明。所提出的可微分显微镜框架补充了设计新相位显微镜系统的创造性过程，并且可以扩展到光学设计中的其他类似应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于可微分显微镜（∂μ）的全光学相位恢复显微镜设计方法，通过数据驱动优化光学系统设计。
AI.motivation: 传统光学系统设计需要大量专业知识和创造力，且效率较低，亟需数据驱动方法辅助设计过程。
AI.method: 提出可微分显微镜框架，采用自上而下的设计策略，结合数据驱动优化算法进行全光学相位恢复显微镜设计，并通过实验验证。
AI.result: 在多个数据集（包括生物样本）上验证了设计的优越性，实验成功证明了其中一种设计的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效补充了光学系统设计流程，未来可拓展至其他光学设计领域。

Ahmad,Azeem Ahmad,Balpreet S. Ahluwalia,Chamira U. S. Edussooriya,Dushan N. Wadduwage physics.optics,cs.CV,physics.comp-ph

authors_text: Haohe Liu,Woosung Choi,Xubo Liu,Qiuqiang Kong,Qiao Tian,DeLiang Wang
title: Neural Vocoder is All You Need for Speech Super-resolution
title_zh: 神经声码器是实现语音超分辨率所需的一切
abstract: Speech super-resolution (SR) is a task to increase speech sampling rate by generating high-frequency components. Existing speech SR methods are trained in constrained experimental settings, such as a fixed upsampling ratio. These strong constraints can potentially lead to poor generalization ability in mismatched real-world cases. In this paper, we propose a neural vocoder based speech super-resolution method (NVSR) that can handle a variety of input resolution and upsampling ratios. NVSR consists of a mel-bandwidth extension module, a neural vocoder module, and a post-processing module. Our proposed system achieves state-of-the-art results on the VCTK multi-speaker benchmark. On 44.1 kHz target resolution, NVSR outperforms WSRGlow and Nu-wave by 8% and 37% respectively on log spectral distance and achieves a significantly better perceptual quality. We also demonstrate that prior knowledge in the pre-trained vocoder is crucial for speech SR by performing mel-bandwidth extension with a simple replication-padding method. Samples can be found in https://haoheliu.github.io/nvsr.
summary: Speech super-resolution (SR) is a task to increase speech sampling rate by generating high-frequency components. Existing speech SR methods are trained in constrained experimental settings, such as a fixed upsampling ratio. These strong constraints can potentially lead to poor generalization ability in mismatched real-world cases. In this paper, we propose a neural vocoder based speech super-resolution method (NVSR) that can handle a variety of input resolution and upsampling ratios. NVSR consists of a mel-bandwidth extension module, a neural vocoder module, and a post-processing module. Our proposed system achieves state-of-the-art results on the VCTK multi-speaker benchmark. On 44.1 kHz target resolution, NVSR outperforms WSRGlow and Nu-wave by 8% and 37% respectively on log spectral distance and achieves a significantly better perceptual quality. We also demonstrate that prior knowledge in the pre-trained vocoder is crucial for speech SR by performing mel-bandwidth extension with a simple replication-padding method. Samples can be found in https://haoheliu.github.io/nvsr.
abstract_zh: 语音超分辨率（SR）是一项通过生成高频分量来提高语音采样率的任务。现有的语音 SR 方法是在受限的实验设置中进行训练的，例如固定的上采样比率。这些强约束可能会导致在不匹配的现实案例中泛化能力较差。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于神经声码器的语音超分辨率方法（NVSR），可以处理各种输入分辨率和上采样率。 NVSR由梅尔带宽扩展模块、神经声码器模块和后处理模块组成。我们提出的系统在 VCTK 多扬声器基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。在 44.1 kHz 目标分辨率下，NVSR 在对数光谱距离上分别优于 WSRGlow 和 Nu-wave 8% 和 37%，并实现了明显更好的感知质量。我们还通过使用简单的复制填充方法执行梅尔带宽扩展，证明了预训练声码器中的先验知识对于语音 SR 至关重要。示例可以在 https://haoheliu.github.io/nvsr 中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经声码器的语音超分辨率方法（NVSR），通过生成高频成分提升语音采样率。
AI.motivation: 现有语音超分辨率方法因固定上采样比例等限制，导致在实际应用中泛化能力不足，需开发更灵活的解决方案。
AI.method: NVSR包含三个模块：梅尔带宽扩展模块、神经声码器模块和后处理模块，并利用预训练声码器的知识通过简单复制填充方法实现梅尔带宽扩展。
AI.result: 在VCTK多说话人基准测试中，NVSR在44.1kHz目标分辨率下分别超越WSRGlow和Nu-wave 8%和37%的对数谱距离，感知质量显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 预训练声码器的先验知识对语音超分辨率至关重要，NVSR的灵活性和性能验证了其在实际场景中的应用潜力。

search_text: Neural Vocoder is All You Need for Speech Super-resolution 神经声码器是实现语音超分辨率所需的一切 Speech super-resolution (SR) is a task to increase speech sampling rate by generating high-frequency components. Existing speech SR methods are trained in constrained experimental settings, such as a fixed upsampling ratio. These strong constraints can potentially lead to poor generalization ability in mismatched real-world cases. In this paper, we propose a neural vocoder based speech super-resolution method (NVSR) that can handle a variety of input resolution and upsampling ratios. NVSR consists of a mel-bandwidth extension module, a neural vocoder module, and a post-processing module. Our proposed system achieves state-of-the-art results on the VCTK multi-speaker benchmark. On 44.1 kHz target resolution, NVSR outperforms WSRGlow and Nu-wave by 8% and 37% respectively on log spectral distance and achieves a significantly better perceptual quality. We also demonstrate that prior knowledge in the pre-trained vocoder is crucial for speech SR by performing mel-bandwidth extension with a simple replication-padding method. Samples can be found in https://haoheliu.github.io/nvsr. 语音超分辨率（SR）是一项通过生成高频分量来提高语音采样率的任务。现有的语音 SR 方法是在受限的实验设置中进行训练的，例如固定的上采样比率。这些强约束可能会导致在不匹配的现实案例中泛化能力较差。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于神经声码器的语音超分辨率方法（NVSR），可以处理各种输入分辨率和上采样率。 NVSR由梅尔带宽扩展模块、神经声码器模块和后处理模块组成。我们提出的系统在 VCTK 多扬声器基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。在 44.1 kHz 目标分辨率下，NVSR 在对数光谱距离上分别优于 WSRGlow 和 Nu-wave 8% 和 37%，并实现了明显更好的感知质量。我们还通过使用简单的复制填充方法执行梅尔带宽扩展，证明了预训练声码器中的先验知识对于语音 SR 至关重要。示例可以在 https://haoheliu.github.io/nvsr 中找到。 本文提出一种基于神经声码器的语音超分辨率方法（NVSR），通过生成高频成分提升语音采样率。 Haohe Liu,Woosung Choi,Xubo Liu,Qiuqiang Kong,Qiao Tian,DeLiang Wang eess.AS,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.SD,eess.SP

authors_text: Yu Du,Fangyun Wei,Zihe Zhang,Miaojing Shi,Yue Gao,Guoqi Li
title: Learning to Prompt for Open-Vocabulary Object Detection with Vision-Language Model
title_zh: 学习使用视觉语言模型提示开放词汇对象检测
abstract: Recently, vision-language pre-training shows great potential in open-vocabulary object detection, where detectors trained on base classes are devised for detecting new classes. The class text embedding is firstly generated by feeding prompts to the text encoder of a pre-trained vision-language model. It is then used as the region classifier to supervise the training of a detector. The key element that leads to the success of this model is the proper prompt, which requires careful words tuning and ingenious design. To avoid laborious prompt engineering, there are some prompt representation learning methods being proposed for the image classification task, which however can only be sub-optimal solutions when applied to the detection task. In this paper, we introduce a novel method, detection prompt (DetPro), to learn continuous prompt representations for open-vocabulary object detection based on the pre-trained vision-language model. Different from the previous classification-oriented methods, DetPro has two highlights: 1) a background interpretation scheme to include the proposals in image background into the prompt training; 2) a context grading scheme to separate proposals in image foreground for tailored prompt training. We assemble DetPro with ViLD, a recent state-of-the-art open-world object detector, and conduct experiments on the LVIS as well as transfer learning on the Pascal VOC, COCO, Objects365 datasets. Experimental results show that our DetPro outperforms the baseline ViLD in all settings, e.g., +3.4 APbox and +3.0 APmask improvements on the novel classes of LVIS. Code and models are available at https://github.com/dyabel/detpro.
summary: Recently, vision-language pre-training shows great potential in open-vocabulary object detection, where detectors trained on base classes are devised for detecting new classes. The class text embedding is firstly generated by feeding prompts to the text encoder of a pre-trained vision-language model. It is then used as the region classifier to supervise the training of a detector. The key element that leads to the success of this model is the proper prompt, which requires careful words tuning and ingenious design. To avoid laborious prompt engineering, there are some prompt representation learning methods being proposed for the image classification task, which however can only be sub-optimal solutions when applied to the detection task. In this paper, we introduce a novel method, detection prompt (DetPro), to learn continuous prompt representations for open-vocabulary object detection based on the pre-trained vision-language model. Different from the previous classification-oriented methods, DetPro has two highlights: 1) a background interpretation scheme to include the proposals in image background into the prompt training; 2) a context grading scheme to separate proposals in image foreground for tailored prompt training. We assemble DetPro with ViLD, a recent state-of-the-art open-world object detector, and conduct experiments on the LVIS as well as transfer learning on the Pascal VOC, COCO, Objects365 datasets. Experimental results show that our DetPro outperforms the baseline ViLD in all settings, e.g., +3.4 APbox and +3.0 APmask improvements on the novel classes of LVIS. Code and models are available at https://github.com/dyabel/detpro.
abstract_zh: 最近，视觉语言预训练在开放词汇目标检测中显示出巨大的潜力，其中在基类上训练的检测器被设计用于检测新类。类文本嵌入首先通过向预训练视觉语言模型的文本编码器提供提示来生成。然后将其用作区域分类器来监督检测器的训练。这一模式成功的关键在于恰当的提示，这需要精心的措辞调整和巧妙的设计。为了避免繁琐的提示工程，针对图像分类任务提出了一些提示表示学习方法，但是当应用于检测任务时，这些方法只能是次优解决方案。在本文中，我们介绍了一种新颖的方法，即检测提示（DetPro），用于基于预训练的视觉语言模型来学习开放词汇对象检测的连续提示表示。与以往面向分类的方法不同，DetPro有两个亮点：1）背景解释方案，将图像背景中的提案纳入提示训练中； 2）上下文分级方案，用于分离图像前景中的提案，以进行定制的提示训练。我们将 DetPro 与 ViLD（一种最新的开放世界物体检测器）组装在一起，并在 LVIS 上进行实验以及在 Pascal VOC、COCO、Objects365 数据集上进行迁移学习。实验结果表明，我们的 DetPro 在所有设置中均优于基线 ViLD，例如，对 LVIS 新型类别的 +3.4 APbox 和 +3.0 APmask 改进。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/dyabel/detpro 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视觉-语言预训练模型的开放词汇目标检测方法DetPro，通过学习连续提示表示来提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有提示工程方法依赖人工设计且在检测任务中效果有限，需自动学习更优的连续提示表示以适应开放词汇检测需求。
AI.method: DetPro包含背景解释方案（整合背景区域）和上下文分级方案（区分前景区域），结合ViLD检测器进行端到端训练。
AI.result: 在LVIS数据集上，DetPro相比基线ViLD在新型类别的APbox和APmask指标分别提升3.4和3.0。
AI.conclusion: DetPro通过针对性的提示学习策略有效提升了开放词汇目标检测性能，验证了方法在多数据集上的有效性。

ai_content.conclusion: DetPro通过针对性的提示学习策略有效提升了开放词汇目标检测性能，验证了方法在多数据集上的有效性。

authors_text: Kaiwen Zhou,Xin Eric Wang
title: FedVLN: Privacy-preserving Federated Vision-and-Language Navigation
title_zh: FedVLN：保护隐私的联合视觉和语言导航
abstract: Data privacy is a central problem for embodied agents that can perceive the environment, communicate with humans, and act in the real world. While helping humans complete tasks, the agent may observe and process sensitive information of users, such as house environments, human activities, etc. In this work, we introduce privacy-preserving embodied agent learning for the task of Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN), where an embodied agent navigates house environments by following natural language instructions. We view each house environment as a local client, which shares nothing other than local updates with the cloud server and other clients, and propose a novel federated vision-and-language navigation (FedVLN) framework to protect data privacy during both training and pre-exploration. Particularly, we propose a decentralized training strategy to limit the data of each client to its local model training and a federated pre-exploration method to do partial model aggregation to improve model generalizability to unseen environments. Extensive results on R2R and RxR datasets show that under our FedVLN framework, decentralized VLN models achieve comparable results with centralized training while protecting seen environment privacy, and federated pre-exploration significantly outperforms centralized pre-exploration while preserving unseen environment privacy.
summary: Data privacy is a central problem for embodied agents that can perceive the environment, communicate with humans, and act in the real world. While helping humans complete tasks, the agent may observe and process sensitive information of users, such as house environments, human activities, etc. In this work, we introduce privacy-preserving embodied agent learning for the task of Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN), where an embodied agent navigates house environments by following natural language instructions. We view each house environment as a local client, which shares nothing other than local updates with the cloud server and other clients, and propose a novel federated vision-and-language navigation (FedVLN) framework to protect data privacy during both training and pre-exploration. Particularly, we propose a decentralized training strategy to limit the data of each client to its local model training and a federated pre-exploration method to do partial model aggregation to improve model generalizability to unseen environments. Extensive results on R2R and RxR datasets show that under our FedVLN framework, decentralized VLN models achieve comparable results with centralized training while protecting seen environment privacy, and federated pre-exploration significantly outperforms centralized pre-exploration while preserving unseen environment privacy.
abstract_zh: 对于能够感知环境、与人类通信并在现实世界中行动的实体主体来说，数据隐私是一个核心问题。在帮助人类完成任务的同时，智能体可能会观察和处理用户的敏感信息，例如房屋环境、人类活动等。在这项工作中，我们为视觉和语言导航（VLN）任务引入了保护隐私的具体化智能体学习，其中具体化智能体通过遵循自然语言指令来导航房屋环境。我们将每个房屋环境视为本地客户端，除了与云服务器和其他客户端共享本地更新外，不共享任何内容，并提出了一种新颖的联合视觉和语言导航（FedVLN）框架，以在训练和预探索期间保护数据隐私。特别是，我们提出了一种去中心化的训练策略，将每个客户端的数据限制在其本地模型训练中，并提出了一种联合预探索方法来进行部分模型聚合，以提高模型对未见过的环境的泛化能力。 R2R 和 RxR 数据集上的大量结果表明，在我们的 FedVLN 框架下，去中心化 VLN 模型在保护可见环境隐私的同时实现了与集中式训练相当的结果，并且联合预探索在保护不可见环境隐私的同时显着优于集中式预探索。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FedVLN框架，在视觉与语言导航任务中实现隐私保护的联邦学习。
AI.motivation: 具身智能体在执行视觉与语言导航任务时可能接触到用户敏感信息，传统集中式训练方式存在隐私泄露风险。
AI.method: 设计联邦学习框架，通过本地训练与部分模型聚合实现隐私保护，包含去中心化训练策略和联邦预探索方法。
AI.result: 在R2R和RxR数据集上，去中心化模型性能接近集中式训练，联邦预探索在隐私保护下显著优于集中式预探索。
AI.conclusion: FedVLN有效平衡了隐私保护与模型性能，在视觉语言导航任务中实现了安全高效的联邦学习方案。

search_text: FedVLN: Privacy-preserving Federated Vision-and-Language Navigation FedVLN：保护隐私的联合视觉和语言导航 Data privacy is a central problem for embodied agents that can perceive the environment, communicate with humans, and act in the real world. While helping humans complete tasks, the agent may observe and process sensitive information of users, such as house environments, human activities, etc. In this work, we introduce privacy-preserving embodied agent learning for the task of Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN), where an embodied agent navigates house environments by following natural language instructions. We view each house environment as a local client, which shares nothing other than local updates with the cloud server and other clients, and propose a novel federated vision-and-language navigation (FedVLN) framework to protect data privacy during both training and pre-exploration. Particularly, we propose a decentralized training strategy to limit the data of each client to its local model training and a federated pre-exploration method to do partial model aggregation to improve model generalizability to unseen environments. Extensive results on R2R and RxR datasets show that under our FedVLN framework, decentralized VLN models achieve comparable results with centralized training while protecting seen environment privacy, and federated pre-exploration significantly outperforms centralized pre-exploration while preserving unseen environment privacy. 对于能够感知环境、与人类通信并在现实世界中行动的实体主体来说，数据隐私是一个核心问题。在帮助人类完成任务的同时，智能体可能会观察和处理用户的敏感信息，例如房屋环境、人类活动等。在这项工作中，我们为视觉和语言导航（VLN）任务引入了保护隐私的具体化智能体学习，其中具体化智能体通过遵循自然语言指令来导航房屋环境。我们将每个房屋环境视为本地客户端，除了与云服务器和其他客户端共享本地更新外，不共享任何内容，并提出了一种新颖的联合视觉和语言导航（FedVLN）框架，以在训练和预探索期间保护数据隐私。特别是，我们提出了一种去中心化的训练策略，将每个客户端的数据限制在其本地模型训练中，并提出了一种联合预探索方法来进行部分模型聚合，以提高模型对未见过的环境的泛化能力。 R2R 和 RxR 数据集上的大量结果表明，在我们的 FedVLN 框架下，去中心化 VLN 模型在保护可见环境隐私的同时实现了与集中式训练相当的结果，并且联合预探索在保护不可见环境隐私的同时显着优于集中式预探索。 本文提出FedVLN框架，在视觉与语言导航任务中实现隐私保护的联邦学习。 Kaiwen Zhou,Xin Eric Wang cs.AI,cs.CL,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Borui Zhang,Wenzhao Zheng,Jie Zhou,Jiwen Lu
title: Attributable Visual Similarity Learning
title_zh: 可归因的视觉相似性学习
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2022. Source code available at https://github.com/zbr17/AVSL
comments: Accepted to CVPR 2022. Source code available at https://github.com/zbr17/AVSL
abstract: This paper proposes an attributable visual similarity learning (AVSL) framework for a more accurate and explainable similarity measure between images. Most existing similarity learning methods exacerbate the unexplainability by mapping each sample to a single point in the embedding space with a distance metric (e.g., Mahalanobis distance, Euclidean distance). Motivated by the human semantic similarity cognition, we propose a generalized similarity learning paradigm to represent the similarity between two images with a graph and then infer the overall similarity accordingly. Furthermore, we establish a bottom-up similarity construction and top-down similarity inference framework to infer the similarity based on semantic hierarchy consistency. We first identify unreliable higher-level similarity nodes and then correct them using the most coherent adjacent lower-level similarity nodes, which simultaneously preserve traces for similarity attribution. Extensive experiments on the CUB-200-2011, Cars196, and Stanford Online Products datasets demonstrate significant improvements over existing deep similarity learning methods and verify the interpretability of our framework. Code is available at https://github.com/zbr17/AVSL.
summary: This paper proposes an attributable visual similarity learning (AVSL) framework for a more accurate and explainable similarity measure between images. Most existing similarity learning methods exacerbate the unexplainability by mapping each sample to a single point in the embedding space with a distance metric (e.g., Mahalanobis distance, Euclidean distance). Motivated by the human semantic similarity cognition, we propose a generalized similarity learning paradigm to represent the similarity between two images with a graph and then infer the overall similarity accordingly. Furthermore, we establish a bottom-up similarity construction and top-down similarity inference framework to infer the similarity based on semantic hierarchy consistency. We first identify unreliable higher-level similarity nodes and then correct them using the most coherent adjacent lower-level similarity nodes, which simultaneously preserve traces for similarity attribution. Extensive experiments on the CUB-200-2011, Cars196, and Stanford Online Products datasets demonstrate significant improvements over existing deep similarity learning methods and verify the interpretability of our framework. Code is available at https://github.com/zbr17/AVSL.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种可归因的视觉相似性学习（AVSL）框架，用于图像之间更准确和可解释的相似性度量。大多数现有的相似性学习方法通​​过使用距离度量（例如马哈拉诺比斯距离、欧几里德距离）将每个样本映射到嵌入空间中的单个点，加剧了不可解释性。受人类语义相似性认知的启发，我们提出了一种广义相似性学习范式，用图来表示两个图像之间的相似性，然后相应地推断出整体相似性。此外，我们建立了自下而上的相似度构建和自上而下的相似度推断框架，以基于语义层次一致性来推断相似度。我们首先识别不可靠的高层相似节点，然后使用最连贯的相邻低层相似节点来纠正它们，同时保留相似性归因的痕迹。在 CUB-200-2011、Cars196 和斯坦福在线产品数据集上进行的广泛实验证明了对现有深度相似性学习方法的显着改进，并验证了我们框架的可解释性。代码可在 https://github.com/zbr17/AVSL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可归因的视觉相似性学习框架（AVSL），旨在提升图像间相似性度量的准确性与可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有相似性学习方法通过单点嵌入和距离度量导致模型不可解释，而人类语义相似性认知更依赖层次化语义关联。
AI.method: 构建基于图的相似性表示，结合自底向上相似性构建与自顶向下层次化推断框架，通过修正高层不可靠节点并保留归因痕迹实现可解释性。
AI.result: 在CUB-200-2011、Cars196和Stanford Online Products数据集上显著优于现有深度相似性学习方法，验证了框架的可解释性。
AI.conclusion: AVSL框架通过层次化语义建模有效提升了相似性度量的准确性与可解释性，为视觉相似性学习提供了新方向。

authors_text: Ali Hatamizadeh,Hamid Hosseini,Niraj Patel,Jinseo Choi,Cameron C. Pole,Cory M. Hoeferlin,Steven D. Schwartz,Demetri Terzopoulos
title: RAVIR: A Dataset and Methodology for the Semantic Segmentation and Quantitative Analysis of Retinal Arteries and Veins in Infrared Reflectance Imaging
title_zh: RAVIR：红外反射成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割和定量分析的数据集和方法
comment: Paper accepted to IEEE Journal of Biomedical Health Informatics (JBHI)
comments: Paper accepted to IEEE Journal of Biomedical Health Informatics (JBHI)
abstract: The retinal vasculature provides important clues in the diagnosis and monitoring of systemic diseases including hypertension and diabetes. The microvascular system is of primary involvement in such conditions, and the retina is the only anatomical site where the microvasculature can be directly observed. The objective assessment of retinal vessels has long been considered a surrogate biomarker for systemic vascular diseases, and with recent advancements in retinal imaging and computer vision technologies, this topic has become the subject of renewed attention. In this paper, we present a novel dataset, dubbed RAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of Retinal Arteries and Veins in Infrared Reflectance (IR) imaging. It enables the creation of deep learning-based models that distinguish extracted vessel type without extensive post-processing. We propose a novel deep learning-based methodology, denoted as SegRAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of retinal arteries and veins and the quantitative measurement of the widths of segmented vessels. Our extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of SegRAVIR and demonstrate its superior performance in comparison to state-of-the-art models. Additionally, we propose a knowledge distillation framework for the domain adaptation of RAVIR pretrained networks on color images. We demonstrate that our pretraining procedure yields new state-of-the-art benchmarks on the DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE_DB1 datasets. Dataset link: https://ravirdataset.github.io/data/
summary: The retinal vasculature provides important clues in the diagnosis and monitoring of systemic diseases including hypertension and diabetes. The microvascular system is of primary involvement in such conditions, and the retina is the only anatomical site where the microvasculature can be directly observed. The objective assessment of retinal vessels has long been considered a surrogate biomarker for systemic vascular diseases, and with recent advancements in retinal imaging and computer vision technologies, this topic has become the subject of renewed attention. In this paper, we present a novel dataset, dubbed RAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of Retinal Arteries and Veins in Infrared Reflectance (IR) imaging. It enables the creation of deep learning-based models that distinguish extracted vessel type without extensive post-processing. We propose a novel deep learning-based methodology, denoted as SegRAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of retinal arteries and veins and the quantitative measurement of the widths of segmented vessels. Our extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of SegRAVIR and demonstrate its superior performance in comparison to state-of-the-art models. Additionally, we propose a knowledge distillation framework for the domain adaptation of RAVIR pretrained networks on color images. We demonstrate that our pretraining procedure yields new state-of-the-art benchmarks on the DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE_DB1 datasets. Dataset link: https://ravirdataset.github.io/data/
abstract_zh: 视网膜血管系统为高血压和糖尿病等全身性疾病的诊断和监测提供重要线索。微血管系统在这种情况下是主要参与的，并且视网膜是唯一可以直接观察到微血管系统的解剖部位。长期以来，视网膜血管的客观评估一直被认为是系统性血管疾病的替代生物标志物，随着视网膜成像和计算机视觉技术的最新进展，该主题已成为重新关注的主题。在本文中，我们提出了一个名为 RAVIR 的新型数据集，用于红外反射 (IR) 成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割。它能够创建基于深度学习的模型，无需进行大量的后处理即可区分提取的血管类型。我们提出了一种基于深度学习的新颖方法，称为 SegRAVIR，用于视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割以及分割血管宽度的定量测量。我们广泛的实验验证了 SegRAVIR 的有效性，并证明了其与最先进模型相比的卓越性能。此外，我们提出了一个知识蒸馏框架，用于 RAVIR 预训练网络在彩色图像上的域适应。我们证明我们的预训练过程在 DRIVE、STARE 和 CHASE_DB1 数据集上产生了新的最先进的基准。数据集链接：https://ravirdataset.github.io/data/

AI.tldr: 本文提出RAVIR数据集和SegRAVIR方法，用于红外反射成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割及定量分析。
AI.motivation: 视网膜血管是高血压、糖尿病等系统性疾病的诊断标志，但传统方法难以精准分割和量化分析，需借助深度学习技术提升效率与准确性。
AI.method: 构建RAVIR红外成像数据集，设计SegRAVIR深度学习模型实现血管分割与宽度测量，并提出知识蒸馏框架将模型迁移至彩色图像领域。
AI.result: SegRAVIR在基准数据集DRIVE、STARE和CHASE_DB1上达到新SOTA，验证了其优于现有方法的性能，且无需复杂后处理。
AI.conclusion: RAVIR数据集与SegRAVIR方法为视网膜血管分析提供新工具，推动系统性疾病的自动化诊断与监测发展。

tldr: 本文提出RAVIR数据集和SegRAVIR方法，用于红外反射成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割及定量分析。
motivation: 视网膜血管是高血压、糖尿病等系统性疾病的诊断标志，但传统方法难以精准分割和量化分析，需借助深度学习技术提升效率与准确性。
method: 构建RAVIR红外成像数据集，设计SegRAVIR深度学习模型实现血管分割与宽度测量，并提出知识蒸馏框架将模型迁移至彩色图像领域。
result: SegRAVIR在基准数据集DRIVE、STARE和CHASE_DB1上达到新SOTA，验证了其优于现有方法的性能，且无需复杂后处理。
conclusion: RAVIR数据集与SegRAVIR方法为视网膜血管分析提供新工具，推动系统性疾病的自动化诊断与监测发展。
search_text: RAVIR: A Dataset and Methodology for the Semantic Segmentation and Quantitative Analysis of Retinal Arteries and Veins in Infrared Reflectance Imaging RAVIR：红外反射成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割和定量分析的数据集和方法 The retinal vasculature provides important clues in the diagnosis and monitoring of systemic diseases including hypertension and diabetes. The microvascular system is of primary involvement in such conditions, and the retina is the only anatomical site where the microvasculature can be directly observed. The objective assessment of retinal vessels has long been considered a surrogate biomarker for systemic vascular diseases, and with recent advancements in retinal imaging and computer vision technologies, this topic has become the subject of renewed attention. In this paper, we present a novel dataset, dubbed RAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of Retinal Arteries and Veins in Infrared Reflectance (IR) imaging. It enables the creation of deep learning-based models that distinguish extracted vessel type without extensive post-processing. We propose a novel deep learning-based methodology, denoted as SegRAVIR, for the semantic segmentation of retinal arteries and veins and the quantitative measurement of the widths of segmented vessels. Our extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of SegRAVIR and demonstrate its superior performance in comparison to state-of-the-art models. Additionally, we propose a knowledge distillation framework for the domain adaptation of RAVIR pretrained networks on color images. We demonstrate that our pretraining procedure yields new state-of-the-art benchmarks on the DRIVE, STARE, and CHASE_DB1 datasets. Dataset link: https://ravirdataset.github.io/data/ 视网膜血管系统为高血压和糖尿病等全身性疾病的诊断和监测提供重要线索。微血管系统在这种情况下是主要参与的，并且视网膜是唯一可以直接观察到微血管系统的解剖部位。长期以来，视网膜血管的客观评估一直被认为是系统性血管疾病的替代生物标志物，随着视网膜成像和计算机视觉技术的最新进展，该主题已成为重新关注的主题。在本文中，我们提出了一个名为 RAVIR 的新型数据集，用于红外反射 (IR) 成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割。它能够创建基于深度学习的模型，无需进行大量的后处理即可区分提取的血管类型。我们提出了一种基于深度学习的新颖方法，称为 SegRAVIR，用于视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割以及分割血管宽度的定量测量。我们广泛的实验验证了 SegRAVIR 的有效性，并证明了其与最先进模型相比的卓越性能。此外，我们提出了一个知识蒸馏框架，用于 RAVIR 预训练网络在彩色图像上的域适应。我们证明我们的预训练过程在 DRIVE、STARE 和 CHASE_DB1 数据集上产生了新的最先进的基准。数据集链接：https://ravirdataset.github.io/data/ 本文提出RAVIR数据集和SegRAVIR方法，用于红外反射成像中视网膜动脉和静脉的语义分割及定量分析。 Ali Hatamizadeh,Hamid Hosseini,Niraj Patel,Jinseo Choi,Cameron C. Pole,Cory M. Hoeferlin,Steven D. Schwartz,Demetri Terzopoulos eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: David Tench,Evan West,Victor Zhang,Michael A. Bender,Abiyaz Chowdhury,J. Ahmed Dellas,Martin Farach-Colton,Tyler Seip,Kenny Zhang
title: GraphZeppelin: Storage-Friendly Sketching for Connected Components on Dynamic Graph Streams
title_zh: GraphZeppelin：动态图流上连接组件的存储友好草图
abstract: Finding the connected components of a graph is a fundamental problem with uses throughout computer science and engineering. The task of computing connected components becomes more difficult when graphs are very large, or when they are dynamic, meaning the edge set changes over time subject to a stream of edge insertions and deletions. A natural approach to computing the connected components on a large, dynamic graph stream is to buy enough RAM to store the entire graph. However, the requirement that the graph fit in RAM is prohibitive for very large graphs. Thus, there is an unmet need for systems that can process dense dynamic graphs, especially when those graphs are larger than available RAM. We present a new high-performance streaming graph-processing system for computing the connected components of a graph. This system, which we call GraphZeppelin, uses new linear sketching data structures (CubeSketches) to solve the streaming connected components problem and as a result requires space asymptotically smaller than the space required for a lossless representation of the graph. GraphZeppelin is optimized for massive dense graphs: GraphZeppelin can process millions of edge updates (both insertions and deletions) per second, even when the underlying graph is far too large to fit in available RAM. As a result GraphZeppelin vastly increases the scale of graphs that can be processed.
summary: Finding the connected components of a graph is a fundamental problem with uses throughout computer science and engineering. The task of computing connected components becomes more difficult when graphs are very large, or when they are dynamic, meaning the edge set changes over time subject to a stream of edge insertions and deletions. However, the requirement that the graph fit in RAM is prohibitive for very large graphs. Thus, there is an unmet need for systems that can process dense dynamic graphs, especially when those graphs are larger than available RAM. We present a new high-performance streaming graph-processing system for computing the connected components of a graph. This system, which we call GraphZeppelin, uses new linear sketching data structures (CubeSketches) to solve the streaming connected components problem and as a result requires space asymptotically smaller than the space required for a lossless representation of the graph. GraphZeppelin is optimized for massive dense graphs: GraphZeppelin can process millions of edge updates (both insertions and deletions) per second, even when the underlying graph is far too large to fit in available RAM. As a result GraphZeppelin vastly increases the scale of graphs that can be processed.
abstract_zh: 查找图的连通部分是整个计算机科学和工程中使用的一个基本问题。当图非常大或动态时，计算连通分量的任务变得更加困难，这意味着边集随着时间的推移而变化，受到边插入和删除流的影响。在大型动态图流上计算连接组件的自然方法是购买足够的 RAM 来存储整个图。然而，对于非常大的图来说，图适合 RAM 的要求是令人望而却步的。因此，对能够处理密集动态图的系统的需求尚未得到满足，特别是当这些图大于可用 RAM 时。我们提出了一种新的高性能流图处理系统，用于计算图的连接组件。这个系统，我们称之为 GraphZeppelin，使用新的线性草图数据结构 (CubeSketches) 来解决流连接组件问题，因此需要的空间渐近小于图无损表示所需的空间。 GraphZeppelin 针对大规模密集图进行了优化：GraphZeppelin 每秒可以处理数百万次边缘更新（插入和删除），即使底层图太大而无法容纳可用 RAM 时也是如此。因此，GraphZeppelin 极大地增加了可处理的图形规模。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GraphZeppelin的高效流式图处理系统，用于在动态图流中计算连通分量，无需将整个图存储在内存中。
AI.motivation: 传统方法要求图数据完全加载到RAM中，但大规模动态图超出内存限制，亟需更节省存储的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用新型线性 sketch 数据结构 CubeSketches，在保证精度的同时显著降低空间复杂度，实现对动态图流的高效处理。
AI.result: 系统可每秒处理数百万条边更新（含插入和删除），即使图规模远超可用内存，大幅提升了可处理图的规模上限。
AI.conclusion: GraphZeppelin 通过创新的 sketching 技术突破了内存瓶颈，为超大规模动态图的连通分量计算提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Sijie Cheng,Zhouhong Gu,Bang Liu,Rui Xie,Wei Wu,Yanghua Xiao
title: Learning What You Need from What You Did: Product Taxonomy Expansion with User Behaviors Supervision
title_zh: 从你所做的事情中了解你需要什么：通过用户行为监管扩展产品分类
abstract: Taxonomies have been widely used in various domains to underpin numerous applications. Specially, product taxonomies serve an essential role in the e-commerce domain for the recommendation, browsing, and query understanding. However, taxonomies need to constantly capture the newly emerged terms or concepts in e-commerce platforms to keep up-to-date, which is expensive and labor-intensive if it relies on manual maintenance and updates. Therefore, we target the taxonomy expansion task to attach new concepts to existing taxonomies automatically. In this paper, we present a self-supervised and user behavior-oriented product taxonomy expansion framework to append new concepts into existing taxonomies. Our framework extracts hyponymy relations that conform to users' intentions and cognition. Specifically, i) to fully exploit user behavioral information, we extract candidate hyponymy relations that match user interests from query-click concepts; ii) to enhance the semantic information of new concepts and better detect hyponymy relations, we model concepts and relations through both user-generated content and structural information in existing taxonomies and user click logs, by leveraging Pre-trained Language Models and Graph Neural Network combined with Contrastive Learning; iii) to reduce the cost of dataset construction and overcome data skews, we construct a high-quality and balanced training dataset from existing taxonomy with no supervision. Extensive experiments on real-world product taxonomies in Meituan Platform, a leading Chinese vertical e-commerce platform to order take-out with more than 70 million daily active users, demonstrate the superiority of our proposed framework over state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our method enlarges the size of real-world product taxonomies from 39,263 to 94,698 relations with 88% precision.
summary: Taxonomies have been widely used in various domains to underpin numerous applications. Specially, product taxonomies serve an essential role in the e-commerce domain for the recommendation, browsing, and query understanding. However, taxonomies need to constantly capture the newly emerged terms or concepts in e-commerce platforms to keep up-to-date, which is expensive and labor-intensive if it relies on manual maintenance and updates. Therefore, we target the taxonomy expansion task to attach new concepts to existing taxonomies automatically. In this paper, we present a self-supervised and user behavior-oriented product taxonomy expansion framework to append new concepts into existing taxonomies. Our framework extracts hyponymy relations that conform to users' intentions and cognition. Specifically, i) to fully exploit user behavioral information, we extract candidate hyponymy relations that match user interests from query-click concepts; ii) to enhance the semantic information of new concepts and better detect hyponymy relations, we model concepts and relations through both user-generated content and structural information in existing taxonomies and user click logs, by leveraging Pre-trained Language Models and Graph Neural Network combined with Contrastive Learning; iii) to reduce the cost of dataset construction and overcome data skews, we construct a high-quality and balanced training dataset from existing taxonomy with no supervision. Extensive experiments on real-world product taxonomies in Meituan Platform, a leading Chinese vertical e-commerce platform to order take-out with more than 70 million daily active users, demonstrate the superiority of our proposed framework over state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our method enlarges the size of real-world product taxonomies from 39,263 to 94,698 relations with 88% precision.
abstract_zh: 分类法已广泛应用于各个领域，以支撑众多应用程序。特别是，产品分类法在电子商务领域的推荐、浏览和查询理解中发挥着重要作用。然而，分类法需要不断捕获电子商务平台中新出现的术语或概念以保持最新，如果依赖于手动维护和更新，这是昂贵且劳动密集型的。因此，我们的目标是分类法扩展任务，将新概念自动附加到现有分类法中。在本文中，我们提出了一种自我监督和面向用户行为的产品分类扩展框架，以将新概念附加到现有分类中。我们的框架提取符合用户意图和认知的上下位关系。具体来说，i）为了充分利用用户行为信息，我们从查询点击概念中提取与用户兴趣匹配的候选下位关系； ii）为了增强新概念的语义信息并更好地检测上下位关系，我们利用预先训练的语言模型和图神经网络与对比学习相结合，通过用户生成的内容以及现有分类法和用户点击日志中的结构信息对概念和关系进行建模； iii）为了降低数据集构建成本并克服数据偏差，我们在没有监督的情况下根据现有分类法构建了高质量且平衡的训练数据集。美团平台是中国领先的外卖垂直电子商务平台，每日活跃用户超过 7000 万，对真实世界产品分类法进行了广泛的实验，证明了我们提出的框架相对于最先进方法的优越性。值得注意的是，我们的方法将现实世界产品分类的规模从 39,263 个关系扩大到 94,698 个关系，精度为 88%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于用户行为监督的自动扩展产品分类体系的框架，通过挖掘用户行为数据中的隐含关系来更新电商领域的分类体系。
AI.motivation: 传统电商分类体系需人工维护新概念，成本高且效率低，需借助用户行为数据自动发现新概念与现有分类的关联关系。
AI.method: 结合预训练语言模型、图神经网络与对比学习，利用用户查询-点击行为提取候选下位词关系，并通过无监督方式构建高质量训练数据集。
AI.result: 在美团平台的真实数据集上，方法将分类关系数量从39,263扩展至94,698，且保持88%的高精度。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有效利用用户行为数据实现分类体系自动化扩展，在精度和规模上优于现有方法，可显著降低人工维护成本。

Specially, product taxonomies serve an essential role in the e-commerce domain for the recommendation, browsing, and query understanding. However, taxonomies need to constantly capture the newly emerged terms or concepts in e-commerce platforms to keep up-to-date, which is expensive and labor-intensive if it relies on manual maintenance and updates. Therefore, we target the taxonomy expansion task to attach new concepts to existing taxonomies automatically. In this paper, we present a self-supervised and user behavior-oriented product taxonomy expansion framework to append new concepts into existing taxonomies. Our framework extracts hyponymy relations that conform to users' intentions and cognition. Specifically, i) to fully exploit user behavioral information, we extract candidate hyponymy relations that match user interests from query-click concepts; ii) to enhance the semantic information of new concepts and better detect hyponymy relations, we model concepts and relations through both user-generated content and structural information in existing taxonomies and user click logs, by leveraging Pre-trained Language Models and Graph Neural Network combined with Contrastive Learning; iii) to reduce the cost of dataset construction and overcome data skews, we construct a high-quality and balanced training dataset from existing taxonomy with no supervision. Extensive experiments on real-world product taxonomies in Meituan Platform, a leading Chinese vertical e-commerce platform to order take-out with more than 70 million daily active users, demonstrate the superiority of our proposed framework over state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our method enlarges the size of real-world product taxonomies from 39,263 to 94,698 relations with 88% precision. 分类法已广泛应用于各个领域，以支撑众多应用程序。特别是，产品分类法在电子商务领域的推荐、浏览和查询理解中发挥着重要作用。然而，分类法需要不断捕获电子商务平台中新出现的术语或概念以保持最新，如果依赖于手动维护和更新，这是昂贵且劳动密集型的。因此，我们的目标是分类法扩展任务，将新概念自动附加到现有分类法中。在本文中，我们提出了一种自我监督和面向用户行为的产品分类扩展框架，以将新概念附加到现有分类中。我们的框架提取符合用户意图和认知的上下位关系。具体来说，i）为了充分利用用户行为信息，我们从查询点击概念中提取与用户兴趣匹配的候选下位关系； ii）为了增强新概念的语义信息并更好地检测上下位关系，我们利用预先训练的语言模型和图神经网络与对比学习相结合，通过用户生成的内容以及现有分类法和用户点击日志中的结构信息对概念和关系进行建模； iii）为了降低数据集构建成本并克服数据偏差，我们在没有监督的情况下根据现有分类法构建了高质量且平衡的训练数据集。美团平台是中国领先的外卖垂直电子商务平台，每日活跃用户超过 7000 万，对真实世界产品分类法进行了广泛的实验，证明了我们提出的框架相对于最先进方法的优越性。值得注意的是，我们的方法将现实世界产品分类的规模从 39,263 个关系扩大到 94,698 个关系，精度为 88%。 本文提出一种基于用户行为监督的自动扩展产品分类体系的框架，通过挖掘用户行为数据中的隐含关系来更新电商领域的分类体系。 Sijie Cheng,Zhouhong Gu,Bang Liu,Rui Xie,Wei Wu,Yanghua Xiao cs.IR,cs.AI

authors_text: Skylar X. Wei,Anushri Dixit,Shashank Tomar,Joel W. Burdick
title: Moving Obstacle Avoidance: a Data-Driven Risk-Aware Approach
title_zh: 移动避障：数据驱动的风险意识方法
comment: This is prepared for IEEE Control Systems Letters (L-CSS) 2022
comments: This is prepared for IEEE Control Systems Letters (L-CSS) 2022
abstract: This paper proposes a new structured method for a moving agent to predict the paths of dynamically moving obstacles and avoid them using a risk-aware model predictive control (MPC) scheme. Given noisy measurements of the a priori unknown obstacle trajectory, a bootstrapping technique predicts a set of obstacle trajectories. The bootstrapped predictions are incorporated in the MPC optimization using a risk-aware methodology so as to provide probabilistic guarantees on obstacle avoidance. We validate our methods using simulations of a 3-dimensional multi-rotor drone that avoids various moving obstacles, such as a thrown ball and a frisbee with air drag.
summary: This paper proposes a new structured method for a moving agent to predict the paths of dynamically moving obstacles and avoid them using a risk-aware model predictive control (MPC) scheme. Given noisy measurements of the a priori unknown obstacle trajectory, a bootstrapping technique predicts a set of obstacle trajectories. The bootstrapped predictions are incorporated in the MPC optimization using a risk-aware methodology so as to provide probabilistic guarantees on obstacle avoidance. We validate our methods using simulations of a 3-dimensional multi-rotor drone that avoids various moving obstacles, such as a thrown ball and a frisbee with air drag.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的结构化方法，让移动代理预测动态移动障碍物的路径，并使用风险感知模型预测控制（MPC）方案来避开它们。给定先验未知障碍物轨迹的噪声测量，自举技术可以预测一组障碍物轨迹。使用风险感知方法将自举预测纳入 MPC 优化中，以便为避障提供概率保证。我们使用 3 维多旋翼无人机的模拟来验证我们的方法，该无人机可以避开各种移动障碍物，例如投掷的球和具有空气阻力的飞盘。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于风险感知的模型预测控制方法，用于动态移动障碍物的路径预测与避障。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理动态障碍物的不确定性及噪声测量时存在不足，难以提供可靠的避障保证。
AI.method: 采用引导技术预测障碍物轨迹，并结合风险感知的MPC优化，以提供概率避障保证。
AI.result: 通过三维无人机仿真验证，成功避开多种动态障碍物，证明方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该数据驱动的风险感知方法在动态障碍物避障中展现出有效性与可靠性。

authors_text: Eric Vollenweider,Marko Bjelonic,Victor Klemm,Nikita Rudin,Joonho Lee,Marco Hutter
title: Advanced Skills through Multiple Adversarial Motion Priors in Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 通过强化学习中的多个对抗性运动先验获得高级技能
abstract: In recent years, reinforcement learning (RL) has shown outstanding performance for locomotion control of highly articulated robotic systems. Such approaches typically involve tedious reward function tuning to achieve the desired motion style. Imitation learning approaches such as adversarial motion priors aim to reduce this problem by encouraging a pre-defined motion style. In this work, we present an approach to augment the concept of adversarial motion prior-based RL to allow for multiple, discretely switchable styles. We show that multiple styles and skills can be learned simultaneously without notable performance differences, even in combination with motion data-free skills. Our approach is validated in several real-world experiments with a wheeled-legged quadruped robot showing skills learned from existing RL controllers and trajectory optimization, such as ducking and walking, and novel skills such as switching between a quadrupedal and humanoid configuration. For the latter skill, the robot is required to stand up, navigate on two wheels, and sit down. Instead of tuning the sit-down motion, we verify that a reverse playback of the stand-up movement helps the robot discover feasible sit-down behaviors and avoids tedious reward function tuning.
summary: In recent years, reinforcement learning (RL) has shown outstanding performance for locomotion control of highly articulated robotic systems. Such approaches typically involve tedious reward function tuning to achieve the desired motion style. Imitation learning approaches such as adversarial motion priors aim to reduce this problem by encouraging a pre-defined motion style. In this work, we present an approach to augment the concept of adversarial motion prior-based RL to allow for multiple, discretely switchable styles. We show that multiple styles and skills can be learned simultaneously without notable performance differences, even in combination with motion data-free skills. Our approach is validated in several real-world experiments with a wheeled-legged quadruped robot showing skills learned from existing RL controllers and trajectory optimization, such as ducking and walking, and novel skills such as switching between a quadrupedal and humanoid configuration. For the latter skill, the robot is required to stand up, navigate on two wheels, and sit down. Instead of tuning the sit-down motion, we verify that a reverse playback of the stand-up movement helps the robot discover feasible sit-down behaviors and avoids tedious reward function tuning.
abstract_zh: 近年来，强化学习（RL）在高度关节机器人系统的运动控制方面表现出了出色的性能。此类方法通常涉及繁琐的奖励函数调整以实现所需的运动风格。诸如对抗性运动先验之类的模仿学习方法旨在通过鼓励预定义的运动风格来减少这个问题。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种方法来增强基于对抗性运动先验的 RL 概念，以允许多种可离散切换的风格。我们证明，即使与无运动数据的技能相结合，也可以同时学习多种风格和技能，而不会出现明显的表现差异。我们的方法在轮腿四足机器人的几个现实世界实验中得到了验证，这些实验展示了从现有 RL 控制器和轨迹优化中学到的技能，例如躲避和行走，以及在四足和人形配置之间切换等新颖技能。对于后一项技能，机器人需要站起来，用两个轮子导航，然后坐下。我们不是调整坐下运动，而是验证站立运动的反向回放有助于机器人发现可行的坐下行为，并避免繁琐的奖励函数调整。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多对抗运动先验的强化学习方法，使机器人能够同时学习并切换多种运动模式，减少对奖励函数的依赖。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习需要大量人工调整奖励函数以实现特定运动风格，而对抗运动先验虽能简化这一过程，但难以支持多种离散切换的运动模式。
AI.method: 通过扩展对抗运动先验框架，引入多模式切换机制，结合现有控制器与轨迹优化技术，在轮腿式四足机器人上实现如蹲下、行走、形态切换（四足/人形）等技能，并利用站立动作的逆向回放学习坐下动作以避免奖励函数调优。
AI.result: 实验验证了该方法在真实机器人上的有效性，能够同时学习多种技能且性能无显著下降，成功实现了形态切换等复杂新技能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过多对抗运动先验有效提升了强化学习的灵活性与实用性，为复杂机器人技能学习提供了无需繁琐奖励调优的解决方案。

authors_text: Jiaming Han,Yuqiang Ren,Jian Ding,Xingjia Pan,Ke Yan,Gui-Song Xia
title: Expanding Low-Density Latent Regions for Open-Set Object Detection
title_zh: 扩展低密度潜在区域以进行开放集目标检测
abstract: Modern object detectors have achieved impressive progress under the close-set setup. However, open-set object detection (OSOD) remains challenging since objects of unknown categories are often misclassified to existing known classes. In this work, we propose to identify unknown objects by separating high/low-density regions in the latent space, based on the consensus that unknown objects are usually distributed in low-density latent regions. As traditional threshold-based methods only maintain limited low-density regions, which cannot cover all unknown objects, we present a novel Open-set Detector (OpenDet) with expanded low-density regions. To this aim, we equip OpenDet with two learners, Contrastive Feature Learner (CFL) and Unknown Probability Learner (UPL). CFL performs instance-level contrastive learning to encourage compact features of known classes, leaving more low-density regions for unknown classes; UPL optimizes unknown probability based on the uncertainty of predictions, which further divides more low-density regions around the cluster of known classes. Thus, unknown objects in low-density regions can be easily identified with the learned unknown probability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method can significantly improve the OSOD performance, e.g., OpenDet reduces the Absolute Open-Set Errors by 25%-35% on six OSOD benchmarks. Code is available at: https://github.com/csuhan/opendet2.
summary: Modern object detectors have achieved impressive progress under the close-set setup. However, open-set object detection (OSOD) remains challenging since objects of unknown categories are often misclassified to existing known classes. In this work, we propose to identify unknown objects by separating high/low-density regions in the latent space, based on the consensus that unknown objects are usually distributed in low-density latent regions. As traditional threshold-based methods only maintain limited low-density regions, which cannot cover all unknown objects, we present a novel Open-set Detector (OpenDet) with expanded low-density regions. To this aim, we equip OpenDet with two learners, Contrastive Feature Learner (CFL) and Unknown Probability Learner (UPL). CFL performs instance-level contrastive learning to encourage compact features of known classes, leaving more low-density regions for unknown classes; UPL optimizes unknown probability based on the uncertainty of predictions, which further divides more low-density regions around the cluster of known classes. Thus, unknown objects in low-density regions can be easily identified with the learned unknown probability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method can significantly improve the OSOD performance, e.g., OpenDet reduces the Absolute Open-Set Errors by 25%-35% on six OSOD benchmarks. Code is available at: https://github.com/csuhan/opendet2.
abstract_zh: 现代物体探测器在近距离设置下取得了令人印象深刻的进步。然而，开放集对象检测（OSOD）仍然具有挑战性，因为未知类别的对象经常被错误分类为现有的已知类别。在这项工作中，基于未知物体通常分布在低密度潜在区域的共识，我们建议通过分离潜在空间中的高/低密度区域来识别未知物体。由于传统的基于阈值的方法仅保留有限的低密度区域，无法覆盖所有未知对象，因此我们提出了一种具有扩展的低密度区域的新型开放集检测器（OpenDet）。为此，我们为 OpenDet 配备了两个学习器：对比特征学习器 (CFL) 和未知概率学习器 (UPL)。 CFL进行实例级对比学习，以鼓励已知类的紧凑特征，为未知类留下更多的低密度区域； UPL根据预测的不确定性优化未知概率，进一步在已知类簇周围划分更多低密度区域。因此，可以利用学习到的未知概率轻松识别低密度区域中的未知物体。大量实验表明，我们的方法可以显着提高 OSOD 性能，例如，OpenDet 在六个 OSOD 基准测试上将绝对开集误差降低了 25%-35%。代码位于：https://github.com/csuhan/opendet2。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进开放集目标检测的方法，通过扩展潜在空间中的低密度区域以更有效地识别未知类别。
AI.motivation: 现有开放集目标检测方法常将未知类别误分类为已知类别，传统阈值方法无法覆盖所有低密度潜在区域，导致检测性能不足。
AI.method: 设计OpenDet框架，包含对比特征学习器（CFL）和未知概率学习器（UPL）。CFL通过实例级对比学习压缩已知类别特征，UPL基于预测不确定性优化未知概率，共同扩展低密度区域。
AI.result: 在六个开放集检测基准测试中，OpenDet将绝对开放集误差降低了25%-35%。
AI.conclusion: 通过扩展低密度潜在区域并结合双学习器机制，该方法显著提升了开放集目标检测对未知类别的识别能力。
